Update architecture diagram via draw.io desktop editor

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement distributed web crawler with 6 microservices

Java 21 / Spring Boot 3.2 / Kafka-based pipeline:
- Seed URL Controller: REST API for submitting crawl seeds
- URL Frontier: politeness-enforced, disk-backed URL queue
- HTML Downloader (x3): virtual thread downloads with robots.txt
- Link Extractor (x2): Jsoup-based link parsing
- URL Filter: extension/domain/depth filtering
- URL Seen: Bloom filter + PostgreSQL deduplication

Includes Docker Compose (14 containers), Prometheus/Grafana
monitoring, unit tests, README, and draw.io architecture diagram.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: restore runtime imports stripped by Phase 8 type-import cleanup

RequiredFieldsForm and CalendarView are JSX components, not TypeScript types.
Marking them as `type` imports causes the bundler to strip them at compile
time, resulting in a ReferenceError at render. Removed the `type` keyword.

commit.message:
feat: font-size 15px, padding 7/22px — читаемость Telegram-уровень в chat-dialog.css

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Drew cursor offset caused by body CSS zoom (0.8 scale factor)

Root cause: body { zoom: 0.8 } causes viewport clientX/clientY to differ
from CSS canvas-local coordinates. Previous offsetX-based approach and the
raw clientX - rect.left approach both failed to account for this scale.

Fix: getPointerPos now divides by (rect.width / canvas.offsetWidth) to
normalize from visual pixels to CSS pixels, making all hit-testing, drawing,
panning, and zoom-pivot calculations consistent.

Also includes Gemini's Drew upgrades: toolbar color swatch replaced with
preset color grid, minor whitespace fixes. Separated showToolbarColor state
from showColorPickers (AI chat theme) to avoid state conflict.

commit.message:
Decomposition scoring v2: compute scores server-side from 6 dimensions

Replace holistic LLM scoring (25% of all scores clustered on 67) with
decomposition approach: Claude estimates 6 factual task-breakdown percentages,
server computes final score via weighted formula with non-linear physical
protection dampening. 93% anchor accuracy in testing (28/30 jobs).

New: server/scoring.js (shared formula + validation)
DB: scoring_version column separates v1/v2 scores
Leaderboard, percentile, score stats filter to v2 only
Scripts updated: rescore.js, seed-seo.js write scoring_version=2

Fix trigger-n8n-workflow to handle empty response bodies.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(server): genericize error messages that leak internal limits

R243-SRV-1 (HIGH): Tenant quota exceeded error disclosed both current
policy count and maximum quota to API clients, enabling capacity
planning for resource exhaustion attacks. Now returns generic message;
numbers logged server-side via tracing::warn.

R243-SRV-2 (MEDIUM): Evidence pack endpoint enumerated all valid
framework names in error response. Now returns generic "Unknown or
unsupported framework".

R243-SRV-3 (MEDIUM): Signup endpoint enumerated all valid plan tiers
in error response. Now returns generic "Invalid plan tier".

R243-SRV-4 (MEDIUM): Evaluation quota exceeded error included used
count and tier limit in the error string that flows into deny reasons
and API responses. Numbers now logged server-side only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(config,review,debate): add tier-based tool selection for review and debate commands (#363)

* feat(config,review,debate): add tier-based tool selection for review and debate commands

When `tier` is configured in [review] or [debate] config sections, the
commands resolve their tool from the specified tier's models list:

- Filter by enabled + binary available
- Prefer heterogeneous model (different ModelFamily from parent tool)
- Use the tier entry's model_spec (carries model name + thinking budget)
- Tier takes priority over explicit `tool` when both are set

Key implementation details:
- TierToolResolution struct and resolve_tool_from_tier() in run_helpers.rs
- resolve_review_tool/resolve_debate_tool return model_spec from tier
- build_executor now applies explicit thinking override even when model_spec
  is present (CLI > config > tier spec precedence)
- Multi-reviewer mode only passes tier_model_spec to the matching reviewer,
  preserving cognitive diversity for heterogeneous reviewers
- Validation ensures tier references exist in config

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
chore: remove dead oscarDBinput write-back feature (#580)

The eForm oscarDBinput write-back feature was already disabled in
AddEForm2Action (replaced with an error log). This removes all the
surrounding dead code that supported the non-functional feature:

- Delete eform_highlight.js (client-side highlight + AJAX refresh)
- Delete FetchUpdatedData2Action.java (server-side AJAX endpoint)
- Remove setupInputFields() and generateInputCode() from EForm.java
- Remove updateFields field from EFormBase.java
- Remove inputMarker and getInputMarker() from EFormLoader.java
- Remove setupInputFields() calls from efmshowform_data.jsp and efmformadd_data.jsp
- Remove dead _oscardodatabaseupdate processing block from AddEForm2Action.java
- Remove now-unused EFormLoader and DatabaseAP imports from AddEForm2Action.java
- Remove FetchUpdatedData Struts action mapping from struts.xml

The oscarDB= read feature (EForm.setDatabaseAPs(), EFormLoader.getMarker())
is unaffected and remains fully functional.

Closes #564

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add session 29 tracks 1-5: BMC, linear consensus, spectral analysis, ATP equilibrium, info theory (203/203)

Track 1: Bounded model checking — 6 protocol models (LCT lifecycle, ATP transfer,
federation membership, trust update, delegation chain, composed LCT+trust),
1477 states explored, 29 properties verified exhaustively (59/59)

Track 2: Linear consensus protocols — HotStuff O(n) vs PBFT O(n²),
trust-weighted leader election, DAG-based consensus, pipelining analysis.
At n=1000, HotStuff uses 250× fewer messages than PBFT (42/42)

Track 3: Trust graph spectral analysis — eigenvalue decomposition, community
detection via Fiedler vector, PageRank, eigenvector centrality, Cheeger
inequality, critical edge/node identification (32/32)

Track 4: ATP economic equilibrium — steady-state circulation simulation,
Gini coefficient tracking, velocity, Lorenz curves, ergodicity testing,
mean reversion analysis, fee rate impact (35/35)

Track 5: Information-theoretic trust bounds — Shannon entropy of trust
distributions, trust attestation as noisy channel, Blahut-Arimoto capacity,
rate-distortion, Fano's inequality error bounds (35/35)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add live 3D character preview to customize screen

Creates a separate Three.js renderer on a canvas beside the colour
swatches. The character rotates slowly and instantly rebuilds when
the player clicks a suit or eye glow swatch, so they can see the
exact look before starting the heist. Preview is torn down cleanly
when the game starts or the player goes back to the menu.

commit.message:
StoryCorps case study: full content rewrite

- challenge: Library of Congress framing + specific failure mode
  ("dropped sessions without warning") + NPR as pressure, not just context
- approach: grandparents/non-tech-adopters constraint named explicitly;
  "forgiving by default" as design principle; React Native constraints sentence
  moved to lead position in concept
- team: "Solo designer · 1 director · 2 engineers · NPR partnership"
- discover: 8+ storytellers with named user types + emotional research focus;
  review analysis now includes the finding (lost recordings, no recovery path);
  NPR sessions framed as brand + user segment research
- design: recording interface names actual decisions (session state, time counter,
  one-tap pause); onboarding names the mechanism (removed sign-up gate);
  component system framed as "one source of truth, both platforms"
- ship: React Native annotations note platform-divergence logic;
  accessibility specifies WCAG AA and 44px target with rationale for older users
- metrics: replaced effort metrics with outcomes —
  "2 platforms, 1 shared component system" + "2 brands unified"
- tech: "Accessibility" → "WCAG AA" (specific standard, not a skill label)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Linux VS Code setup: run as user instead of admin

Debian home directories are 700 by default, so admin can't access
the user's vm-tools clone. Run via vm_exec_user instead — the user
has NOPASSWD sudo for the systemd setup commands.

Fix text-white on colored buttons in light mode

The global [data-theme=light] .text-white rule remapped all white text
to near-black (#0f172a) including CTA buttons with colored backgrounds.

- CSS: scoped the remap to :not(button):not(a) so buttons and links
  keep their white text in light mode

  a belt-and-suspenders fix

pipe-separated text instead of proper HTML tables.

repository.description: Zack's Tiling Window Manager

commit.message:
Implement primitive interface, registry, and ripple-wave tick engine

Primitive package: interface with Process/Subscriptions/Cadence/Lifecycle,
5 mutation types (AddEvent, AddEdge, UpdateState, UpdateActivation,
UpdateLifecycle) with visitor pattern, thread-safe Registry with lifecycle
state machine, PrimitiveState snapshots.

Tick engine: ripple-wave processor with layer-ordered execution (sequential
between layers, parallel within), cadence gating, subscription-based event
matching, wave limit safety valve, and atomic mutation application.

Coverage: primitive 95.5%, tick 90.1%. 34 tests, all passing with -race.
